title: lupinus polyphyllus subsp. arcticus
intro: Lupinus polyphyllus subsp. arcticus, also known as the arctic lupine, is a subspecies of the perennial lupine plant native to North America. It has medicinal properties due to its high content of alkaloids, particularly lupanine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the arctic lupine is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
